title: COPD exacerbation with steroid plan and antibiotic threshold

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How has your breathing changed from usual?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: More breathless for two days and using my puffer more. Sputum is more but still white.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any chest pain, blue lips, confusion, fever, blood in sputum, or oxygen at home?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No chest pain or blood. No blue lips. I had 37.7. No home oxygen.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Your oxygen level is 94%, respiratory rate 22, wheeze but no crackles. This is a mild COPD flare.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Not today because sputum is not purulent and you are stable. Take prednisolone 50 mg daily for 5 days, increase salbutamol via spacer, and start antibiotics only if sputum turns green or fever develops. Review in 48 hours or sooner if worse.

candidate_notes.note: COPD exacerbation: increased dyspnoea and sputum volume x2 days, sputum white. No chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, confusion, or home oxygen. T 37.7, SpO2 94%, RR 22, wheeze no crackles. Plan: prednisolone 50 mg daily x5 days, increase salbutamol via spacer. No antibiotics today; start if sputum purulent/green or fever. Review 48h; urgent if worsening breathlessness, cyanosis, confusion, chest pain, haemoptysis.
COPD flare with increased breathlessness and sputum. Prescribed prednisolone and antibiotics. Review if worse.

S: COPD, more breathless x2 days, more white sputum, no chest pain/haemoptysis/cyanosis/confusion. O: SpO2 94, RR 22, wheeze no crackles. A: Mild COPD exacerbation. P: Prednisolone 50 mg daily 5 days, salbutamol via spacer, antibiotics if purulent sputum or fever.

Mild COPD exacerbation. Stable: SpO2 94%, RR 22, no chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, confusion, no home O2. White sputum so no antibiotics today. Prednisolone 50 mg daily x5 days, salbutamol via spacer. Antibiotics if sputum becomes purulent/green or fever. Review 48h, urgent if worsening breathlessness or red flags.

CDSS COPD pathway: increased breathlessness/sputum but white sputum and stable observations. No severe features. Treat with prednisolone 50 mg daily for 5 days and increased bronchodilator via spacer. Antibiotic threshold: purulent/green sputum or fever. Review in 48h; escalate for cyanosis, confusion, chest pain, haemoptysis, worsening dyspnoea.
